category.description: v2: policy-engine scope family. Authoritative for scopePolicyEngine categorization (admin/users -> admin; ai_agent -> ai; default banking).

uri.description: v2: the resource server's audience (PingOne aud). Authoritative — bootstrap creates the RS with this audience.

scopes.description: Scopes native to this resource server (this RS is the canonical owner).

mirroredScopes.description: v2: scopes mirrored onto this RS because it is an RFC 8693 exchange-hop audience. Per ARCHITECTURE-TRUTHS T-10, scope vocabularies are per-resource and do not cascade — a scope traveling through an exchange hop must exist on that hop's audience resource. These are owned (native) by another RS but must also exist here.

type.description: v2: PingOne application type bootstrap should create.

grantTypes.description: v2: OAuth grant types for this app (e.g. authorization_code, client_credentials, token_exchange).

isResourceServer.description: v2: if this app IS a resource server (its inbound token aud target), the resource name it corresponds to. Used to wire app -> RS audience.

servers.description: v2: per-runtime-service model — which resource the service owns/validates and what it gates on. Documents the deployment topology so bootstrap + audits derive server expectations from one place.

resource.description: The resource server whose audience this service validates inbound tokens against.

validatesAudience.description: The aud value this service requires on inbound tokens (normally the resource's uri).

gatesOnToolScopes.description: True if this service enforces per-tool requiredScopes (e.g. the MCP Gateway). Implies every gateway-surface tool scope must be mirrored onto this server's resource.
